title: Adobe CJM ExperienceEvent - Custom Channel Context
description: Per-event channel identity for outbound custom-channel deliveries (such as Viber, Kakao, and other customer-created channels). Present alongside `customExecutionMetrics` when `customExecutionMetrics.executionType` is `channel`. Carries identifiers for the three layers of a custom channel's setup: the channel template (Layer 1 â€” `customChannelID` / `customChannelVersion`), the API credentials (Layer 2 â€” `customChannelAuthID` / `customChannelAuthName`), and the channel configuration (Layer 3 â€” `customChannelConfigurationID` / `customChannelConfigurationName`). Other channel families (email, SMS, push) use their own channel-context field groups and leave this absent.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Custom Channel ID
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Layer-1 identifier â€” the slug that identifies the channel template this delivery used (for example, `viber`, `kakao`, or another customer-chosen slug). Stable for the lifetime of the registered custom channel. Used as the primary identifier for filtering custom-channel reporting; the slug also serves as the human-readable display name in reports.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Custom Channel Version
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Layer-1 Custom Channel template version identifier (e.g. `v1_line`, `v1_viber`). Used to distinguish reports across template evolution; e.g., separating pre-template-bump and post-template-bump metrics. Stable for a given publication of the Custom Channel template.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: API Credentials ID
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Layer-2 identifier â€” the unique ID of the specific API credentials used for this delivery. Distinct from `customChannelID` (the Layer-1 template); many credential entries can exist for one channel template. Intended for future support drilldown (for example, identifying which credentials produced a failure) and is not currently surfaced as a reporting filter. May be empty when the delivery path did not involve the API-credentials layer.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: API Credentials Name
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Layer-2 display name for the API credentials (for example, `Viber Marketing Bot â€” APAC`), chosen when the credentials were created. Intended for future support drilldown and is not currently surfaced as a reporting filter; may be empty until the upstream name lookup is available.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Custom Channel Configuration ID
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Layer-3 identifier â€” the unique ID of the channel configuration (the marketer-facing usage policy) selected for this campaign or journey. Distinct from `customChannelAuthID` (Layer-2 credentials); many configurations can reference the same credentials. Intended for future marketer-facing drilldown and is not currently surfaced as a reporting filter. May be empty when the delivery path did not involve the channel-configuration layer.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Custom Channel Configuration Name
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Layer-3 display name for the channel configuration (for example, `Viber Holiday Marketing Config`), chosen when the configuration was created. Intended for future marketer-facing drilldown. May be empty when the delivery path did not involve the channel-configuration layer.
